exports.name: computeCurrentStreak
exports.signature: computeCurrentStreak(days: Array<{date: string, contribution_count: number}>) => {streak_days: number, anchor_date: string|null}
exports.behavior: Counts trailing consecutive days with contribution_count >= 1, starting from the most recent day in `days` and walking backward. If the most recent day has 0 contributions but the day before has >=1, the streak is counted ending at that prior day (per github-readme-streak-stats convention, today-with-zero does not break the streak yet). If `days` is empty or no day has any contributions, returns {streak_days: 0, anchor_date: null}.

exports.name: computeMostUsedLanguage
exports.signature: computeMostUsedLanguage(repos: Array<{is_fork: boolean, languages_bytes: Object}>) => {language: string|null, bytes: number, share_pct: number}
exports.behavior: Sums bytes per language across all repos with is_fork=false. Returns the language with the largest byte total, the byte count, and its share of total bytes as a percentage (0-100, rounded to 1 decimal). If no eligible repos or all language maps empty, returns {language: null, bytes: 0, share_pct: 0}.

exports.name: sliceLast90Days
exports.signature: sliceLast90Days(days: Array<{date: string, contribution_count: number, weekday: number}>, today_utc: string) => Array<{date: string, contribution_count: number, weekday: number}>
exports.behavior: Returns the trailing 90 calendar days ending at today_utc (inclusive). today_utc is a YYYY-MM-DD string in UTC. If `days` covers fewer than 90 days, pads the leading edge with zero-contribution entries so the result is always length 90. Ordering: ascending by date.

invariants: sliceLast90Days output is always exactly length 90 (padding with zero days at the leading edge if necessary).
computeCurrentStreak never throws — empty input → zero streak.
computeMostUsedLanguage excludes forks (per A7) and is deterministic for ties (alphabetical secondary sort).
rationale: Three derivation functions match the three derived metrics on the card. Each is pure to make edge-case testing trivial and deterministic.
